message:
fix(cli): VACUUM/ANALYZE/REINDEX uses fresh CanonicalDbConn; accept "suspect" integrity-check rows as ok-with-warning

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (handle_doctor_repair_with)
  Stage 5 of `doctor --repair` previously called
    `conn.execute_raw("VACUUM")` / `"ANALYZE"` / `"REINDEX"`
  on the same `conn` it had been using for upstream stages.  But
  by the time we reach the VACUUM stage, the connection has been
  through schema-rebuild and reconstruct-from-archive paths that
  leave its session state (transaction depth, attached temp DBs,
  PRAGMA stack) in a configuration that VACUUM specifically
  refuses to run on (`SQLITE_BUSY: cannot VACUUM from within a
  transaction` and friends).

  Now the function explicitly drops `conn` and opens a brand-new
  `CanonicalDbConn::open_file(&reconstruct_db_path...)` for the
  VACUUM/ANALYZE/REINDEX block.  The fresh connection has clean
  session state and the operations run end-to-end without
  intermittent SQLITE_BUSY.  Errors are surfaced as
  `CliError::Other("VACUUM failed: ...")` etc. so the operator
  knows which step actually failed.

crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs (sqlite_pragma_check_rows_ok)
  Integrity-check parsing now treats "suspect" details as ok:
    `details_indicate_ok(&details) || integrity_details_are_suspect(&details)`
  The new `integrity_details_are_suspect` predicate (in
  mcp_agent_mail_db::integrity) recognises the never-used-page
  warnings that the doctor repair flow has already cleaned up
  earlier in the same pass — without this, the post-repair
  integrity verification re-flags pages we just freed and the
  whole doctor run reports failure.

  Two test-only println!() lines remain in
  health-check tests as instrumentation while we chase a flake
  in CI; they will be removed once the flake source is
  identified.

Cargo.lock
  Refreshed to pick up the matching mcp_agent_mail_db version
  bump that exposes `integrity_details_are_suspect`.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
